comment: dark-factory M17 enterprise tier: a CONSERVATIVE Docker seccomp profile. defaultAction is SCMP_ACT_ALLOW (this is NOT a default-deny allowlist profile like Docker's default.json) -- it denies a specific, named set of syscalls that are either kernel-integrity-sensitive (mount/umount2, kernel-module load/unload, kexec, reboot) or namespace/inspection primitives that could be used to weaken or observe past the container barrier (ptrace, bpf, setns, unshare, swapon). See dark-factory/references/enterprise.md for the honest-scope note: this is a documented starting point, not a hand-tuned per-role profile (that is a named, deliberate deferral).
